purpose: Services we KNOW exist on pay.sh but the agent cannot end-to-end use today. Four distinct sub-categories — check the `reason` and the matching `reasons.<code>` entry to know which: (1) CANNOT PAY (protocol/auth) — mpp_protocol / siwx_auth_required / invalid_demand. agent_pay fails at the protocol layer or refuses zero-demand. (2) CAN PAY BUT CANNOT DELIVER — requires_binary_response. agent_pay WOULD succeed and spend USDC, but the response (PNG/audio/video) can't be piped to Telegram/Discord as an attachment yet. (3) NOT 402 AT PROBE — endpoint_not_402_at_probe. Service is listed upstream but our probe got a non-402 status (4xx/5xx/200/301) — broken, moved, or auth-gated differently. (4) UNVERIFIED RESPONSE SHAPE — unverified_paid_response_shape. agent_pay WOULD settle, but available evidence about the response shape is contested or absent (e.g. openapi declares one content-type while product-family inference suggests another). We hold out conservatively until we capture a real paid response. Either way: NEVER call agent_pay on these. For (1) and (3) it fails for free. For (2) and (4) it would burn the user's USDC on output we can't promise we can deliver. Tell the user the service exists, explain the gap, and (if relevant) reference the BAT tracking the fix or suggest a re-probe. Several entries also carry a `note` documenting BAT-706 audit findings — sibling endpoints on the same host that ARE payable but aren't catalogued yet (deferred to BAT-708 for per-endpoint description + safety scoping).

mpp_protocol.label: Multi-Party Protocol (MPP)
mpp_protocol.explanation: Service uses pay.sh's newer Multi-Party Protocol settlement flow. Our X402Protocol implementation only supports the standard x402 v2 single-signer flow. Adding MPP support is non-trivial — separate BAT, not yet filed.

siwx_auth_required.label: Sign-In-With-X auth required
siwx_auth_required.explanation: Service requires a wallet auth handshake (sign-in-with-Solana style) before it will return a 402 with payment requirements. We don't implement that auth pre-flight yet. Tracked-adjacent in BAT-697 (Trigger V2 also needs SIWX) — once SIWX lands there, these become payable.

invalid_demand.label: Service returns 402 with amount=0 (no demand)
invalid_demand.explanation: Service returns HTTP 402 with amount=0 — semantically 'free' from x402's perspective, but still a 402 status. agent_pay refuses zero-demand by design, AND our web_fetch tool throws on any non-2xx response (so it can't fetch these either). Net result: not currently usable via our tools. Possible follow-up: a tool that fetches with 402-tolerant semantics, or a flag on web_fetch to allow 402 pass-through.

requires_binary_response.label: Service expected to return binary content (image/audio/video) that we can't deliver
requires_binary_response.explanation: agent_pay parses x402 settles and returns the response body as best-effort UTF-8 text. Services expected to return binary content (PNG screenshots, AI-generated images, audio, video) can be paid but the binary bytes can't currently be piped to the Telegram/Discord channel as an attachment — there's no in-memory-buffer-to-channel path. Tracked: needs a follow-up BAT to (a) save binary content to a workspace file and (b) plumb that file path to the channel sender. Each service's `note` field documents the EVIDENCE BASIS for the classification: 'paid-response-observed' (we have a captured paid response confirming binary), 'product-family-inference' (the service's published product is image/audio/video gen — e.g. fal.ai Flux/SDXL/Stable-Video — making binary the only plausible response shape, AND no openapi evidence contradicts it), or 'openapi-response-content-type' (the openapi spec declares image/* / video/* / audio/* media types). Distinct from `unverified_paid_response_shape`: a service goes there if the evidence is contested (e.g. openapi declares application/json while product family suggests binary), as happened with paysponge/screenshotone in PR #378 R10. Operational consequence: refuse the service to avoid spending USDC for output the channel can't deliver.

endpoint_not_402_at_probe.label: Service exists in pay.sh's catalog but didn't return a 402 at probe time
endpoint_not_402_at_probe.explanation: Service is listed in pay.sh's upstream catalog but our probe got a non-402 HTTP status (4xx/5xx, sometimes 200/301). Possible causes: endpoint moved, broken, auth-gated differently, or re-routed to a free response. agent_pay needs a 402 to settle, so it can't pay these. web_fetch could in theory reach a 2xx response, but the agent doesn't know the request shape and shouldn't probe blindly. Each entry's `note` field records the probe-time HTTP status as a hint. Tell the user the service is listed upstream but not currently reachable through our tooling. (Note: for some entries the BAT-706 audit later found OTHER payable endpoints on the SAME host that the catalog URL didn't point at — see the per-entry note.)

unverified_paid_response_shape.label: Paid endpoint exists and parses OK, but the response shape isn't yet verified — we can't promise the channel can render the output
unverified_paid_response_shape.explanation: agent_pay would settle (we have a parseable 402 with Solana-USDC), but we have NOT captured an actual paid response, and the available evidence is contested (e.g. openapi declares one content-type while product-family inference suggests another). Conservative refuse: a paid call could burn USDC on output the channel can't render (binary bytes with no file-pipe path), OR could succeed with JSON whose envelope shape we haven't documented. Different from `requires_binary_response`, which we only assert when evidence clearly points to binary content (paid-response-observed or unambiguous openapi media types). Deferred to BAT-708 for paid-response capture + classification before catalog inclusion.

services.name: paysponge/screenshotone
services.note: ScreenshotOne is a screenshot-as-a-service product (publicly documented at screenshotone.com); PRODUCT-FAMILY INFERENCE alone suggested binary content (PNG/GIF/JPEG/MP4) on /animate and /take. HOWEVER, the captured 402 openapi for the catalog endpoint DECLARES application/json — see tests/paysh/captures/catalog/paysponge-screenshotone.json (resource.mimeType: "application/json", bazaar output.type: "json"). That CONTRADICTS the binary-by-family inference: the paid response could be a URL- or base64-wrapped JSON envelope we CAN deliver to a channel, or a JSON shape we haven't documented. We have NOT settled a paid call to observe the actual response body. Conservative refuse — deferred to BAT-708 for paid-response capture before catalog inclusion. Updated 2026-05-15 (PR #378 R10) to RETRACT the earlier requires_binary_response classification, which rested on family inference and didn't reconcile with the openapi evidence.

services.name: paysponge/coingecko
services.note: http_404 at 2026-05-14 probe — endpoint removed; replaced upstream by stablecrypto/market-data which is in catalog.json.

services.name: paysponge/fal
services.note: BAT-706 audit (2026-05-15) confirmed 9 paid endpoints on fal.x402.paysponge.com all return parseable 402 challenges for Solana-USDC: /fal-ai/fast-sdxl ($0.01), /fal-ai/flux/dev ($0.03), /fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1 ($0.04), /fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1-ultra ($0.06), /fal-ai/flux/schnell ($0.01), /fal-ai/minimax/video-01 ($0.07), /fal-ai/recraft-v3 ($0.04), /fal-ai/stable-diffusion-v35-large ($0.04), /fal-ai/stable-video ($0.07). Evidence basis for the binary classification: PRODUCT-FAMILY INFERENCE — the endpoint names map to fal-ai's published image- and video-generation models (Flux SDXL, Recraft v3, Stable Diffusion v3.5, Stable Video, Minimax-video) which return PNG/MP4 binary content by their nature. The audit confirms the 402-and-build path works; we have NOT settled a payment and inspected the response body. Follow-up path to upgrade evidence_basis: extract declared response content-types from fal's openapi.json (the audit's fetcher reaches it successfully — see catalog-audit.md enumerating 30 endpoints from it; WebFetch with a different user-agent gets HTTP 403, but probe-catalog.js's UA works). Parsing openapi response.content media types per endpoint would let us flip this to evidence_basis: openapi-response-content-type. Until then we refuse to avoid spending USDC on output the channel can't deliver. See tests/paysh/catalog-audit.md for the parsed_ok evidence (run 2026-05-15 with --audit-side-effects).

services.name: paysponge/perplexity
services.note: Catalog-listed endpoint returned http_200 at 2026-05-14 probe — non-402, agent_pay can't settle that URL. BAT-706 audit (2026-05-15, --audit-side-effects) discovered 3 SIBLING endpoints on the same host that DO return parseable Solana-USDC 402 challenges: /search ($0.01), /v1/agent ($0.01), /v1/async/sonar ($0.01). See tests/paysh/catalog-audit.md for the parsed_ok evidence. These are payable today but NOT yet catalogued — they need per-endpoint description (request body shape, response shape verification) before catalog inclusion. Deferred to BAT-708. Until then, the agent should tell users that perplexity is listed upstream and the catalog URL itself doesn't pay, but BAT-708 will add searchable Perplexity endpoints to the catalog.

services.name: paysponge/nyne
services.note: Catalog-listed endpoint returns 402 amount=0 (free-from-x402's-perspective, refused by agent_pay design). BAT-706 audit (2026-05-15, --audit-side-effects) discovered 2 SIBLING endpoints on the same host that DO have demand and parse OK for Solana-USDC: /person/enrichment ($0.02) and /person/search ($0.02). See tests/paysh/catalog-audit.md. These are payable today but NOT yet catalogued — person-enrichment/search is privacy-sensitive (PII lookup of third parties) and needs explicit safety scoping (authorized-use-only pattern, like 2captcha and textbelt-sms in catalog.json) before catalog inclusion. Deferred to BAT-708.
